summary: Matched most of lbfile by replacing the deleted asm/melee/lb/lbfile.s object with C implementations in src/melee/lb/lbfile.c, removing lbfile.s from obj_files.mk, and adding support fixes in DVD headers and sbss alignment. The C version covers file-load callback/cancel handling, filename extension selection for .usd/.dat based on lbLang, DVD entry lookup/open/length queries, HSD_DevComRequest reads, and archive allocation/cache paths. One small function, lbFile_800161A0, remained as inline asm under NON_MATCHING guards. A review comment explicitly justified removing DVDFileInfo.callback because the struct layout was needed for Dolphin DVD stack matches and likely came from a different SDK version.

key_files.role: Main C implementation replacing lbfile.s; added file callback/cancel handling, extension construction, DVD file length/open wrappers, async DevCom reads, and archive loading helpers.
Deleted 678-line assembly file after matching in C.
Removed asm/melee/lb/lbfile.s.o so the build uses src/melee/lb/lbfile.c.o.
Adjusted DVDFileInfo layout by removing callback field and made DVDConvertPathToEntrynum take const char*.
Added .balign 8 before sbss symbol lbl_804D63D0, likely to preserve small-BSS layout after lbfile changes.

what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/lb/lbfile.s and removed its object from obj_files.mk, indicating the lbfile text range was now produced from C instead of standalone assembly.
Added C definitions for func_800161C4, func_80016204, func_8001634C, func_800163D8, func_800164A4, func_80016580, func_8001668C, func_80016760, and func_800168A0 in src/melee/lb/lbfile.c.
Kept lbFile_800161A0 as inline asm in the C file under NON_MATCHING fallback, wrapped with #pragma push/#pragma pop and clang-format off/on comments.
Made cancel a static BOOL in lbfile.c and changed lbFile_8001615C to assert on cancelflag using the literal string "!cancelflag", then set cancel = TRUE.
Introduced static char lbl_803BA508[] = __FILE__ and a static filename buffer lbl_80432058 with constants MAX_FILENAME_LENGTH, FILE_EXTENSION_LENGTH, and MAX_BASENAME_LENGTH.

Implemented func_80016204 to append or preserve file extensions: existing non-empty extensions are kept, a trailing dot uses lbLang_IsSettingUS, and no extension uses lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS; appended extensions are "usd" or "dat".
Implemented DVD length helpers using DVDConvertPathToEntrynum, DVDFastOpen, DVDFileInfo.length, DVDClose, OSDisableInterrupts, OSRestoreInterrupts, OSReport, and __assert.
Implemented HSD_DevComRequest paths with 0x20-byte rounding via (*size + 0x1F) & 0xFFFFFFE0 and transfer type selection based on whether an HSD_Archive pointer is at or above 0x80000000.
Changed DVDConvertPathToEntrynum prototype from char* to const char*.
Added .balign 8 to asm/melee/lb/lbunknown_005.s before lbl_804D63D0 in .sbss.
smart_moves: Matched broad lbfile behavior in C while isolating the remaining hard-to-match lbFile_800161A0 as inline asm instead of keeping the whole module in assembly.
Used real source-level control flow for file extension logic and DVD error handling, preserving OSReport and __assert line-number-sensitive calls such as 0x99, 0xD8, 0xEE, and 0x11A.
Updated the Dolphin DVD struct layout globally rather than papering over stack offsets locally; the author noted this was needed for other dolphin/dvd stack matches too.
Preserved static data placement by defining lbl_803BA508 from __FILE__, making cancel static, and adding explicit .sbss alignment in a neighboring asm file.
Kept const-correctness for DVDConvertPathToEntrynum once lbfile began passing const filename pointers.
Used a small inline helper, qwer, and an inline wrapper for func_800163D8 to reproduce generated code shape for repeated archive-load paths.
decomp_lessons: SDK header layouts can be version-sensitive: a field that exists in one Dolphin SDK definition may break stack matches for the target binary. Validate struct size and offsets against callsite stack behavior.
When converting a module from asm to C, remove the old asm object from obj_files.mk in the same PR to avoid duplicate text and ensure the matched C object is linked.
If one tiny function remains stubborn, inline asm inside the C file can let the rest of the module decompile cleanly without blocking object-file replacement.
Small-BSS and alignment side effects matter across files; matching one module may require .balign changes in adjacent asm to preserve global layout.
For file-size reads through DVDFileInfo, field offsets are decisive. Here DVDFileInfo.length at 0x34 was used by stack reads, and the removed callback field changed the expected struct footprint.
Pointer-region checks like ((u32) ptr >= 0x80000000) can be needed to reproduce transfer mode constants passed to engine I/O APIs.
Preserve assert strings and line-number constants when matching functions with OSReport/__assert paths; these calls strongly constrain codegen and rodata.
naming_conventions: Address-based placeholder names were retained for unknown functions: func_800161C4, func_80016204, func_8001634C, func_800163D8, func_800164A4, func_80016580, func_8001668C, func_80016760, and func_800168A0.
Known callback-like symbols used lbFile_ prefix: lbFile_8001615C and lbFile_800161A0.
Generated-label-style data names were retained where exact identity/layout mattered: lbl_803BA508, lbl_80432058, and lbl_804D63D0.
Local constants used uppercase descriptive names for inferred sizes: MAX_FILENAME_LENGTH, FILE_EXTENSION_LENGTH, and MAX_BASENAME_LENGTH.
The helper name qwer is non-semantic and appears to be a matching expedient rather than a reusable naming convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the standalone lbfile.s asm file after adding C equivalents, but preserved lbFile_800161A0 as inline asm with nofralloc.
Wrapped inline asm with #pragma push/#pragma pop and disabled clang-format around the asm block to keep formatting tooling from disturbing it.
Used an otherwise unused local variable in func_80016580, likely to influence stack/register allocation for matching.
Used inline functions qwer and func_800163D8_inline to encourage inlining/code-shape matching in callsites.
Used exact bit masking ((*arg2 + 0x1F) & 0xFFFFFFE0) to reproduce 0x20-byte alignment for read sizes.
Added .balign 8 in .sbss of lbunknown_005.s to maintain global alignment after converting lbfile.
Removed DVDFileInfo.callback so stack offsets and frame sizes for DVDFastOpen/DVDClose callers match the target.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: This is needed for some dolphin/dvd stack matches as well, so I think it's correct. I assume this struct def was copied from a different dolphin sdk version.
review_feedback.lesson: The DVDFileInfo.callback removal was intentional and based on stack matching evidence, not just local lbfile convenience.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Approved with no body in the available review data.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbfile placeholder functions such as func_80016204 or func_800163D8.
Search for other Dolphin DVD stack-match fixes involving DVDFileInfo layout or removed callback fields.
Look for future cleanup of inline asm lbFile_800161A0 into matching C.
Search for other PRs that add .balign in asm files after moving neighboring modules from asm to C.
Check whether qwer was later renamed or eliminated once surrounding archive functions were better understood.
